data.title: Rio Tinto extracts first gallium from alumina refining in partnership with Indium
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) said Wednesday it extracted its first primary gallium as part of a partnership with Indium Corp. that aims to eventually produce commercial quantities of the critical, rare earth metal from bauxite processed at Rio's Vaudreuil alumina refinery in Quebec.

The preliminary step was performed at Indium's research and development facility located in Rome, New York, with the next phase of the project involving the assessment of extraction techniques to enable the production of larger quantities of gallium at pilot-scale.

If successful, Rio (NYSE:RIO) said it plans to build a demonstration plant in Saguenay-Lac-Saint-Jean, financially supported by the government of Quebec, with a capacity of up to 3.5 metric tons/year of gallium.

If Rio (RIO) succeeds in taking production to commercial scale, the miner anticipates output of as much as 40 metric tons/year, representing 5%-10% of the current global gallium production.

The news comes as China restricts exports of metals such as gallium, germanium and antimony to the U.S. as part of the trade war between the two countries.

data.title: Storebrand ASA reports interim Q1 results
data.description:
• Storebrand ASA press release (OTCPK:SREDF): Q1 group profit of NOK 1,167 million, strong growth in savings and insurance
  • Operational result of NOK 800M, up 16% compared to Q1 2024.
  • Combined ratio of 97%, down from 98% in Q1 2024, maintaining 90-92% ambition for 2025.

data.title: Kinross gold targets $650M in shareholder returns for 2025 through dividends and buybacks
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Kinross Gold Corporation (KGC) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Paul Rollinson highlighted strong Q1 performance with production of 512,000 ounces, driven by Tasiast and Paracatu, which accounted for over half of total production and cash flow. He confirmed that Tasiast remains on track to meet its 2025 production guidance despite a fire-related shutdown in April.
  • Rollinson announced progress in growth projects, including underground development at Round Mountain Phase X, resource drilling at Curlew, and baseline studies at Lobo-Marte. Great Bear also advanced with detailed engineering and exploration.
  • CFO Andrea Freeborough reported Q1 adjusted earnings of $0.30 per share and free cash flow of $371 million. She emphasized lower costs of sales and all-in sustaining costs compared to the prior quarter. The company repurchased $60 million in shares to date and aims to return $650 million in capital to shareholders in 2025.
  • Freeborough noted improvements in the balance sheet, with net debt reduced to $540 million and a net debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 0.2 times. The company plans to repurchase $500 million in shares this year, supported by strong cash flow.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed full-year production guidance of 2 million ounces at a cost of sales of $1,120 per ounce and all-in sustaining costs of $1,500 per ounce. CapEx guidance remains at $1.15 billion.
  • Freeborough stated that operating costs are expected to rise due to changes in the accounting treatment for certain mining activities, inflation, and production sequencing. Inflation is projected at 3%-4% for the year.
  • Rollinson confirmed the reactivation of the share buyback program, targeting a minimum of $500 million in repurchases for 2025, alongside a quarterly dividend of $0.03 per share.

Financial Results

  • Q1 production totaled 512,000 gold equivalent ounces, with sales of 506,000 ounces. The cost of sales was $1,038 per ounce, and all-in sustaining costs were $1,355 per ounce.
  • Adjusted operating cash flow was $676 million, and free cash flow excluding working capital changes reached $472 million.
  • Tasiast achieved production of 138,000 ounces at $811 per ounce in costs, while Paracatu produced 147,000 ounces at $951 per ounce. La Coipa and U.S. assets met production and cost expectations.
  • The company ended Q1 with $695 million in cash and $2.3 billion in liquidity. The term loan was fully repaid during the quarter.

Q&A

  • Anita Soni, CIBC: Asked about Tasiast's restart timeline following the fire. COO Claude Schimper confirmed a three-week shutdown, including additional maintenance work.
  • Tanya Jakusconek, Scotiabank: Inquired about Great Bear's permitting progress. EVP Geoff Gold stated that all necessary permits for current activities are in place, with additional permits expected later in the year. Negotiations with First Nations remain constructive.
  • Carey MacRury, Canaccord Genuity: Questioned the share buyback program's dependency on gold prices. CFO Freeborough indicated strong commitment to the $500 million target under current market conditions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts demonstrated a positive tone, commending strong Q1 results and management's clear guidance. They focused on operational resilience and shareholder returns.
  • Management displayed confidence in achieving guidance and advancing strategic projects. Rollinson emphasized the company's robust financial position and disciplined approach.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained consistent with a focus on operational excellence and capital returns.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Production in Q1 2025 was slightly higher than Q4 2024, supported by strong contributions from Tasiast and Paracatu.
  • Cost of sales and all-in sustaining costs were lower in Q1, reflecting higher production and cost efficiencies.
  • Management's tone remained optimistic, with continued focus on shareholder returns and growth initiatives. Analysts maintained a positive outlook, consistent with the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Potential risks include inflationary pressures, changes in accounting treatments increasing operating costs, and project execution challenges.
  • Analysts raised questions about permitting timelines for Great Bear and the operational impact of the Tasiast fire.

Final Takeaway

Kinross Gold Corporation delivered strong Q1 2025 results, supported by robust production and cost efficiencies. Management reaffirmed annual guidance and emphasized shareholder returns through dividends and a $500 million share buyback program. Progress in strategic growth projects, including Great Bear and Round Mountain Phase X, positions the company well for sustained future production. Analysts showed confidence in Kinross's operational and financial strategies, with management maintaining a disciplined approach to capital allocation and project execution.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Apple's Cue says iPhone maker exploring adding AI search as queries fall
data.description:
• Eddy Cue, Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) senior vice president of services, said on Tuesday that the company is exploring the option of adding artificial intelligence search options to its Safari web browser.
  • Cue, who made the comments at Google's (GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) antitrust trial, added that Perplexity AI and Anthropic, maker of the Claude chatbot, could be options. Searches in Apple's Safari browser fell for the first time in April, Cue added.
  • At stake is a significant chunk of high-margin revenue for Apple, with Google widely believed to have $20B to the iPhone maker in 2022 to be the default search option in Safari. 
  • As generative AI chatbots such as ChatGPT, Claude and others become more prevalent, some have expressed concern about the dominance of Google's search market share. During recent testimony at the trial, Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai said he expects to sign an agreement with Apple by the middle of this year to include its AI service Gemini on new iPhones.
  • Apple shares erased earlier gains and fell 0.5% following Cue's comments, while Google shares fell 4%.

data.title: CVS Health’s Q1 beat prompts Glenview Capital to lower stake
data.description:
Activist investor Glenview Capital Management has reduced its ownership stake in CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) after the pharmacy chain operator raised its full-year earnings outlook, posting better-than-expected Q1 2025 financials early this month.

The New York-based investment firm, which convinced CVS (NYSE:CVS) management to add four new members to the company’s board in November, said it has offloaded 3.75M CVS shares, indicating a reduction of a third of its stake.

“Our position size afforded us the scale necessary to constructively engage with the Board, Management, and fellow Shareholders in Q3 2024 on a multi-faceted performance improvement plan,” Glenview Capital said in a press release late Tuesday.

The firm added that CVS shares have returned 25% in total since its purchases in May 2024, “reflecting initial progress in the long-term cultural and operational turnaround underway at the company.”

“Our confidence in the near-, medium-, and long-term outlook for CVS remains strong,” the hedge fund noted, adding that CVS remains its largest position and the company doesn’t intend to make further adjustments to its holdings.

data.title: Fluidra reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Y/YFluidra, S.A. press release (OTCPK:FLUIF): Q1 net income of €48M was up 29% year-on-year. 
  • Revenue of €564M (+7% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA was up 10% year-on-year to €131M, representing a 23% margin, with consistent gross margin improvement due to the Simplification Program partially offset by higher Opex.

data.title: Shift4 Payments further extends tender offer to acquire Global Blue
data.description:
Payment processor Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) announced on Wednesday a further extension of its all-cash tender offer to acquire the tax-free shopping platform Global Blue Holding (NYSE:GB).

FOUR agreed to buy tax-free shopping platform Global Blue for $7.50 per share in cash and a roughly $2.5B enterprise value in February.

The tender offer to purchase all of the outstanding shares of GB will remain open until one minute after 11:59 PM New York City time on May 20, but may be extended again while the parties await certain regulatory approvals.

Global Blue's board unanimously recommends that all shareholders tender their shares into the offer.

The transaction is expected to close by the third quarter.

data.title: MasterCraft defies tough conditions with FQ3 profit beat
data.description:
MasterCraft Boat Holdings (NASDAQ:MCFT) weathered a very tough macroeconomic environment in their fiscal third quarter and managed to earn a profit that beat Wall Street’s expectations by as much as 12 cents per share, driving the stock up 10% in Wednesday's trade. 

 “Our business performed well during the third quarter against a backdrop of macroeconomic and demand uncertainty,” noted CEO Brad Nelson, adding that the company’s capital allocation priorities remain “disciplined and consistent despite external pressures.” This, coupled with a debt-free balance sheet, will enable the company to provide a stable backdrop amid market and tariff uncertainties, while its variable operating model allows it to “swiftly adjust production as needed.”

For the fiscal third quarter, adjustments made to dealer inventory to align with the softer retail environment resulted in a 9.5% drop in sales to $76M, slightly better than Wall Street anticipated. Profits were also less than last year, dropping by 40% to $0.30 per share, but topped $0.18 expectations. Accordingly, the changes to sales price, material and overhead inflation narrowed the company’s gross margin by 250 basis points to 20.8%.

Looking ahead to FY25, the “evolving” macroeconomic conditions and a “challenging demand environment” led the company to revise its full year top and bottom-line guidance. Net sales are now expected to be between $275M versus prior estimates to be between $275M to $295M and is less than the consensus estimate of $285.6M.

Adjusted earnings are now expected to be $0.71 per share from prior guidance of $0.64 to $0.86 per share, 2 cents less than expectations. Adjusted EBITDA was revised to $20M from earlier expectations to be between $19M and $24M, while capital expenditures have been modified to $9M from $12M, previously.
